blocks.text: Used at the end of direction-giving sentences: following the directions will lead you to the destination — like "and you're there / then you'll arrive."

blocks.vi: Quầy sữa ở đâu? → Anh đi thẳng rồi rẽ trái là tới.
blocks.en: Where is the dairy section? → Go straight, then turn left, and you'll get there.

blocks.vi: Đi thẳng, rẽ phải là tới.
blocks.en: Go straight, turn right, and you'll arrive.

blocks.vi: Em đi tới đèn xanh đèn đỏ là tới.
blocks.en: Reach the traffic lights and you'll arrive.

blocks.text: "tới" = arrive/reach; "là" connects the directions to the result — literally "...[do this], then [you] arrive." A casual, natural way to end directions in spoken Vietnamese.
